1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the five-phase build-out plan. After iter-071's three-track prover round (−7 net sorries, the largest single-iter delta since iter-064), the sorry landscape is:
18	
19	| File | Sorries | Phase | Status |
20	|---|---|---|---|
21	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | **6** | A | iter-072 Lane 4 |
22	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | **8** | B | iter-072 Lane 3 |
23	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | **1** | C/D (consolidated) | iter-072 Lane 1 (refactor) |

27	
28	Iter-071's structural highlight was the `JacobianWitness` single-witness consolidation pattern on `Jacobian.lean`, which collapsed five originally-protected sorries into one unprotected existence hypothesis. This unblocks Phase E at the type-checking level — `Jacobian C` is now well-typed for both genus branches, so `AbelJacobi.lean`'s three sorries are ready for closure.

30	## Current Objectives
31	
32	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
33	
34	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` — Phase C consolidation refactor (precursor to Lane 2)
35	
36	**File status.** 1 syntactic sorry, 228 LOC. Theorem under attack: `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (L162). Iter-071 prover bundled the five originally-protected sorries (`Jacobian` g>0 branch, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`) into a single existence hypothesis via the `JacobianWitness` structure, with the four protected instances projecting the relevant field of `(jacobianWitness C)`.
37	
38	**Iter-072 target.** **Refactor — do not attempt to close the existence sorry**. The `JacobianWitness` structure currently fixes an internal marked point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` as a field, with `isAlbanese : @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred` indexed on that internal `P`. This is incompatible with `AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` (whose `P` is an arbitrary input). Refactor as follows:
39	
40	1. **Remove the `P` field from `JacobianWitness`.** The bundle should no longer carry a marked point.
41	2. **Parameterise the `isAlbanese` field on `P`**:
42	   ```
43	   isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
44	   ```
45	   so that the same Albanese object `J` serves as Albanese for every choice of pointing. This is mathematically correct: the *scheme* `J` is intrinsic; only the universal morphism depends on `P`.
46	3. **`nonempty_jacobianWitness` is unchanged in signature** (`Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) := sorry`), since `P` is no longer a parameter of the bundle.
47	4. **`jacobianWitness C` unchanged** (`Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)`).
48	5. **The four protected instances are unchanged in proof** — each still projects the relevant field of `jacobianWitness C` (which no longer has a `P` field but still has `grpObj`, `proper`, `smooth`, `geomIrred`, `smoothGenus`, `J`).
49	6. **`Jacobian C` unchanged** — its g>0 branch still projects `(jacobianWitness C).J`.
50	
51	Verify the single remaining sorry is `nonempty_jacobianWitness` and that all four protected instances still discharge. Document the new shape in your task result so Lane 2 can rely on it.
52	
53	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Jacobian.tex` (§ *The Albanese universal property*, *The Jacobian definition*, *Existence of an Albanese variety*).
54	
55	**Hard constraints.** Protected signatures (`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`) frozen. No new axioms. **Do not attempt `nonempty_jacobianWitness`** — it is the deferred Phase C existence claim (no symmetric powers, no FGA representability in current Mathlib).

57	### 2. `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` — Phase E unblocked
58	
59	**File status.** 3 syntactic sorries, 57 LOC. Three protected declarations, all bare `:= sorry` / `by sorry`:
60	
61	| Decl | Line | Type |
62	|---|---|---|
63	| `Jacobian.ofCurve` | L34 | `(P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C) → C ⟶ Jacobian C` |
64	| `Jacobian.comp_ofCurve` | L39 | `P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C]` |
65	| `Jacobian.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` | L49 | Albanese factorisation existence + uniqueness |
66	
67	**Iter-072 target.** Close all three sorries via case-split on `genus C = 0` + witness projection. **Precondition**: this iteration's Lane 1 (`Jacobian.lean` prover) is concurrently refactoring `JacobianWitness` to parameterise `isAlbanese` on `P`. Write your code against the **target post-refactor shape**:
68	
69	- `JacobianWitness C` has no `P` field.
70	- `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J` — a forall-indexed Albanese predicate.
71	- The four protected instances on `Jacobian C` are independent of the choice of `P`.
72	
73	If Lane 1's refactor has not yet landed when your edits compile, your edits will reference field/method names that do not exist; the compile error will be informative and iter-073 will resolve. The architectural decision (parameterised `isAlbaneseFor`) is fixed.
74	
75	**Strategy per declaration.**
76	
77	- **`ofCurve P`** (L34). Two cases:
78	  - `genus C = 0`: `Jacobian C = 𝟙_ _` (terminal). The unique morphism `C ⟶ 𝟙_ _` works; for `Over (Spec (.of k))` this is `toUnit C` (the unique map to the terminal object).
79	  - `genus C ≠ 0`: `Jacobian C = (jacobianWitness C).J`. Use `((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve : C ⟶ (jacobianWitness C).J`.
80	  - Recall `noncomputable def ofCurve` is allowed under the 2026-05-07 user authorisation.
81	
82	- **`comp_ofCurve P`** (L39). Two cases:
83	  - `genus C = 0`: every morphism into the terminal object is unique, so `P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C]` by uniqueness. Use `Unique.eq_default` or `Subsingleton.elim` on `Hom (𝟙_ _) (𝟙_ _)` (one-element, identity).
84	  - `genus C ≠ 0`: `((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve : P ≫ ((isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve) = η[(jacobianWitness C).J]`.
85	
86	- **`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp P f hf`** (L49). Albanese factorisation:
87	  - `genus C = 0`: any `g : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ A` is unique (the unique map *out* of the terminal object is constrained iff `A` has a unique map *from* `𝟙_ _`, which holds when `A` has a global section). Actually, for `A` with `[GrpObj A]`, the neutral element `η[A] : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ A` is the canonical choice; uniqueness follows from `hf : P ≫ f = η[A]` and the genus-0 collapse making `f = η[A] ∘ (terminal map)`. Concretely: `g := η[A]`, `hg_eq : f = ofCurve P ≫ g` by computing both sides on the terminal object.
88	  - `genus C ≠ 0`: `((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf` (using the iter-069 `IsAlbanese.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` extractor).
89	
90	The `IsAlbanese` namespace already contains the three extractors (`ofCurve`, `comp_ofCurve`, `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`) — see `Jacobian.lean` L61-75.

93	- For the terminal-object branch: `CartesianMonoidalCategory.toUnit`, `Limits.IsTerminal.uniqueUpToIso`, `Subsingleton.elim` on terminal-object Hom-sets.
94	- For `[GrpObj A]` and `η[A]`: `MonObj.one_def`, `η[]` notation defined in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group`.
95	
96	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelJacobi.tex`.

104	**Iter-072 primary target.** **Close `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` inner sorry at L242.** The skeleton's `desc` call needs a `Derivation' φ_g'` of the pushed-forward target `M_pushed := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))`. 106	```
107	d_target : M_pushed.Derivation' φ_g' := {
108	  d := fun U b => D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)

The substantive obligation is `d_app`: prove that for `a ∈ Y.presheaf U`, `D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a)) = 0`. This is an instance of the `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` naturality across `f ≫ g`, combined with `D_X.d_app`. The relevant identity is:

121	  = (φ_fg'.app (.op (f⁻¹U.unop))) ∘ (canonical pullback transport map)

147	### 4. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — Phase A continuation

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus

8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (iteration 069 — Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The genus-0 case is closed: `Jacobian C = 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` (the terminal object),
16	which is `Spec k` with its identity morphism. For `g > 0`, the definition reduces to the
17	Albanese construction (deferred).
18	
19	The file now contains:
20	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
21	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
22	- Decomposition of the five protected sorries into genus-0 / genus-pos cases.
23	
24	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
25	
26	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
27	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
28	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
29	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
30	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`.
31	
32	The iteration-069 resolution is a *conditional* use of the terminal object: it is used
33	exactly when `genus C = 0`, which is the mathematically correct answer for that case.
34	-/
35	
36	set_option autoImplicit false
37	
38	universe u
39	
40	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
41	
42	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
43	
44	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
45	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
46	  [IsProper C.hom]
47	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
48	
49	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
50	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
51	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
52	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C) (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
53	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
54	  ∃ (ι : C ⟶ J), P ≫ ι = η[J] ∧
55	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
56	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]),
57	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = ι ≫ g
58	
59	namespace IsAlbanese
60	
61	noncomputable def ofCurve {C P J} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
62	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
63	  Classical.choose h
64	
65	lemma comp_ofCurve {C P J} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
66	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
67	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
68	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
69	
70	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {C P J} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
71	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
72	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
73	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
74	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
75	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf
76	
77	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
78	universal morphisms. -/
79	theorem unique {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
80	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
81	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
82	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
83	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
84	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
85	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
86	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
87	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
88	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
89	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
90	    simp
91	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
92	    refine (hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_).symm
93	    rw [Category.assoc, hg_eq, hh_eq]
94	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
95	  have ⟨h₂, hh₂_eq, hh₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
96	  have h₂_eq_id : h₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
97	    refine (hh₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
98	    simp
99	  have hhg_eq_h₂ : h ≫ g = h₂ := by
100	    refine (hh₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_).symm
101	    rw [Category.assoc, hh_eq, hg_eq]
102	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_h₂, h₂_eq_id]
103	  use g
104	  constructor
105	  · exact hg_eq
106	  · intro g' hg'
107	    exact (hg_unique g' hg').symm
108	
109	end IsAlbanese
110	
111	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
112	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
113	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
114	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) := by
115	  constructor
116	  intro x K _ y Z fst snd h
117	  have : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
118	  rw [ObjectProperty.prop_iff_of_iso (IrreducibleSpace ·) (asIso snd)]
119	  infer_instance
120	
121	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
122	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
123	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), a marked $k$-rational point
124	`P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, and a proof that `(J, P)` satisfies the Albanese universal property
125	of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C`. The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific
126	relative dimension `genus C`, which is the form required by
127	`Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
128	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
129	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
130	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
131	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
132	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
133	  grpObj : GrpObj J
134	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
135	  proper : IsProper J.hom
136	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
137	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
138	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
139	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
140	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
141	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
142	  /-- The marked $k$-rational point of `C`. -/
143	  P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C
144	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data for `(C, P, J)`. -/
145	  isAlbanese : @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
146	
147	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
148	curve. This is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C Jacobian
149	scaffolding: it packages all five protected sorries (`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`,
150	`smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`)
151	into a single existence hypothesis.
152	
153	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
154	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
155	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
156	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
157	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
158	existence is assumed and the proof is deferred to a future iteration. -/
159	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
160	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
161	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) :=
162	  sorry
163	
164	/-- A choice of Albanese witness for `C`, extracted via `Classical.choice`.
165	Used to populate the genus `> 0` branch of `Jacobian C` and to discharge each of
166	the four protected instances. -/
167	noncomputable def jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
168	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
169	    JacobianWitness C :=
170	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)
171	
172	-- data
173	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
174	
175	For genus `0`, the Jacobian is the terminal object `Spec k` (the trivial group scheme).
176	For genus `> 0`, the Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C`
177	(see `JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
178	witness is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C scaffolding. -/
179	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
180	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
181	  if h : genus C = 0 then
182	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))

input.old_string:
/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), a marked $k$-rational point
`P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, and a proof that `(J, P)` satisfies the Albanese universal property
of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C`. The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific
relative dimension `genus C`, which is the form required by
`Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
  grpObj : GrpObj J
  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
  proper : IsProper J.hom
  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
  smooth : Smooth J.hom
  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
  /-- The marked $k$-rational point of `C`. -/
  P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C
  /-- The Albanese universal-property data for `(C, P, J)`. -/
  isAlbanese : @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
input.new_string:
/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.

Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
an *arbitrary* input `P`.

The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/

## Summary

Iter-072 Lane 1 was a **structural refactor of `JacobianWitness`** (not a sorry
closure). The single remaining sorry — `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (L172) — is
the Phase-C existence claim and is explicitly off-limits this iteration.

**Sorry count**: 1 → 1 (unchanged, as planned).

## Refactor performed

The `JacobianWitness` bundle previously carried an internal marked point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`
as a field, with `isAlbanese` indexed on that specific `P`. This shape is
incompatible with `AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P`, whose `P` is an *arbitrary*
input — Lane 2 needed the Albanese property to be uniform over `P`.

The refactor (per PROGRESS.md §1):

1. **Dropped the `P` field** from the `JacobianWitness` structure.
2. **Renamed `isAlbanese` → `isAlbaneseFor`** and changed its type from
   ```
   isAlbanese : @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
   ```
   to
   ```
   isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
   ```
3. **`nonempty_jacobianWitness` signature unchanged** — still
   `Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) := sorry`. Per PROGRESS.md "hard constraints",
   this sorry was not touched.
4. **`jacobianWitness` unchanged** — still `Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)`.
5. **Four protected instances unchanged** — they project `grpObj`, `smoothGenus`,
   `proper`, `geomIrred` from the witness, none of which were renamed or
   re-typed. They remain `P`-independent (which is mathematically correct: the
   group/proper/smooth/irreducible properties of the underlying scheme `J` do
   not depend on the choice of pointing).
6. **`Jacobian C` unchanged** — its `g > 0` branch still projects
   `(jacobianWitness C).J`, which is `P`-independent.

## Post-refactor shape (precondition for Lane 2)

For any input `(C, P)`, Lane 2 can extract the Albanese morphism uniformly via:

```
let w := jacobianWitness C
let hP := w.isAlbaneseFor P    -- : IsAlbanese C P w.J
hP.ofCurve                      -- : C ⟶ w.J
hP.comp_ofCurve                 -- : P ≫ hP.ofCurve = η[w.J]
hP.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf
                                -- : ∃! g : w.J ⟶ A, f = hP.ofCurve ≫ g
```

Combined with the `Jacobian C` g>0 branch reducing to `w.J`, the four protected
instances on `Jacobian C` ensure type-correctness for the `IsAlbanese C P (Jacobian C)`
statement that Lane 2 needs.

- **Field name discoverability**: `isAlbaneseFor` is the documented new name in

  introduce new imports, instance synthesis, or universe-level constraints.
